dataList.dlg: Ooh... Is that Ssanghwa-cha I smell? It's got a strong aroma and taste today, I like it. Look at that, the egg yolk's sitting right in the center again—very nice.

Clocked in early this morning, eh, Sinclair? I thought I announced a late start today, what with yesterday's contract going late into the night and all.
Morning, Frau Faust. Did the Thousand Needles contract wrap up all right?

Feel free to grab lunch outside—I'll just scrounge something up here at the office. Hm? Yeah, I just need to go over the logistics of our next contract. Know what I'm talking about, right? It's about this invitation.

Ooooooof... I can't even stay seated for long at my age. Days like this, stuck behind a desk, my back feels like it's about to give out by the time dinner rolls around. How about you call it here and head home too, Frau Faust? I'll help you track down where that resonator came from. You need not be concerned about me, Herr Gregor. - No, I mean... I can't just lock the place up and head home with you still sitting here, can I?
Hm? Oh, this obituary? Pay it no mind. I was never that fond of this "friend," anyway. Foolish old pal... Always saw things so one-dimensionally.

During those days... The luxury of Ssanghwa-cha this fragrant, or steam this gentle, was beyond anything I could imagine. I only remember the stench of smoke that burned my eyes and throat... and the smell of humans—in a bad way.

Honor, status... You two better not get too hung up on stuff like that. I can't quite put it into words, but the more I wonder about what keeps the City prosperous, a strange sense of dreadful nausea settles in. 
I don't remember every detail of what happened, or what I saw, but the body remembers what it experienced. Whatever I can't verbalize can't be linked to anything good, no sir.
Say, why don't you think about how many people you have killed so far... and ask yourself whether your life is worth more than theirs?

An old body holds as much experience as it has aged. You know what they say, don't you?
"It's not the strong who survive... it's the survivors who are strong."
*sip* *sigh* ... Man, that boy's a Color Fixer-level when it comes to brewing tea...

... *puff* Well, I hope you weren't looking forward to a peaceful end after imbruing your hands in so much blood.
Gird your loins, rookie. It's about to get real.
Ready, team?
Then let's enter.
Don't waste time worrying about me—focus inward. You wouldn't want to miss the things that really matter.
Like the first light...
Hm, you must've gotten tired.
Just gotta... power through it with a good oomph.
What a feeble bunch.
There's... no need for all of us... to share the same fate.
Did you really need an old guy like me to do this for you, Kid?
Uhm... Boy, I can never get the hang of all this new tech stuff.

I pay much respect to your courage—takes a lot to keep on fighting, especially after those first few clashes must've taught you how outclassed you were.
The wounds you've left on this little Office family have taught us a valuable lesson.

Must've been a bad day for these fellas or something... I wouldn't have hesitated to take on this contract had I known it would be this easy.
<color=#ffb1b4>Shall we secure the Books of other individuals while the opportunity permits?</color>
Sure, why not. Might as well make some extra cash while we're here. Ah, and do make sure to collect every last Zwei book. Those'll fetch a pretty penny.
I always knew... it would end this way. I've no attachment to life, but... I'm simply worried about the kid's future...
